title: RFE: podman load: provide diagnostics on failure
body:
I'm struggling to generate a tarball that `podman load` will handle, but am stymied by unhelpful errors:
```
# podman load --input /tmp/old.tar 
Failed
error pulling "dir:/tmp/old.tar": unable to pull dir:/tmp/old.tar
```
Here `old.tar` is an archive generated using a very old docker; the problem is almost certainly related to [differences between the docker v1 and v1.1 image spec](https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/master/image/spec/README.md); podman can't handle v1. I'm trying to handcraft a `manifest.json` that will work in docker 1.9, 1.10, and podman. It would help me tremendously to know the reason podman rejects my attempts.

Note that `--log-level debug` adds nothing of value; note also that strace indicates the tarball is being unpacked and read.

Would it be possible (and easy) for podman to provide an informative diagnostic describing its reason for failing?

comments.body: Far as I can trace, we are printing the full error returned by containers/image; I think better debug info from the library would definitely be helpful.
https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/blob/27107fdac1d75f97caab47cd13efb1d9900cf350/libpod/image/image.go#L141 is swallowing the underlying error report.
(At least partially, I need to go through pullImage and make sure we forward all the errors along there)
@mheon is this fixed completely or should I go through it again to ensure all errors are forwarded?
@umohnani8 I think this is fixed, going to close.
